summary: Clear soundboard capture of a mellow ’69 show highlighted by an electrifying Alligator / Caution jam, stellar Lovelight and an epic Easy Wind jam.
review: The performance is widely praised for its relaxed yet inspired vibe, with standout moments like the Alligator→Caution jam, a soaring Lovelight, a long Easy Wind improvisation and strong vocal duets on Next Time. Recording quality is generally good – later tracks are crisp and intimate, though the first five songs suffer from muffling and occasional hiss. Overall the show shines despite some vocal mix quirks and early‑track sound issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 5 stars and highlight multiple classic performances, while a minority note recording flaws and vocal problems; the weighted average of show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Later portions are clear, crisp and well‑balanced; early tracks (first five) are muffled, hissy and lower fidelity. Some reviewers mention vocals too high in the mix and occasional off‑key moments.

standout_songs: Uncle John's Band > He Was A Friend Of Mine
poor_songs: He Was A Friend Of Mine (vocals ragged/off‑key)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows from a mellow first half into a powerful second half; the extended Easy Wind jam and the Alligator→Caution sequence provide high energy, while the early songs are more subdued but still enjoyable.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work is praised as sweet and expressive; vocals receive mixed feedback – some call them pristine, others note they sound low or out of key.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass contributions are highlighted during the Alligator/Caution jam, with occasional wrap‑around jams noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Organ/keyboard work (Tom Constanten) is described as sweet and integral to the jam sections.
